topic: China SG null (2009) — Kim & Chang 2018 DOI .024→.022 verification (PATCH from ISS-1999 self-apply)
change_summary: ISS-1999 self-apply: detail_evidence DOI '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024' (Sadeghi & Emami paper) corrected to '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022' (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049). Narrowness gate per closed-issues: 1 digit, 2 files (wins.json + sections.json), no logic change.
our_argument_summary: WIN-014 retains the Refuted-by-Data verdict structure from c7: the dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal applied to documented eclipse-magnetic perturbations (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble, 1991–2016, 207 measurements at 100 INTERMAGNET observatories) predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–9 µGal at the 2009 Chinese SG sites; the network recorded zero across all sites. This verification confirms the Kim & Chang citation now resolves correctly — the .022 DOI matches the PII (S0273117718300656) Kim & Chang 2018 paper. Defense-neutralization arguments still funnel into Section 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster.

relevant_files: data/wins.json:014 (detail_evidence, paragraph 3 of 4 — the Kim & Chang DOI string)
data/sections.json (part6 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster — same Kim & Chang citation)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py code path. The DOI fix is a pure string substitution; no derivation or computation changed.
data_sources_used: Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — verified via web search (ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K, first-author code K = Kim; topic of geomagnetic field variations during solar eclipses, 39 eclipses 1991–2016, 207 measurements, 100 observatories — exactly what our detail_evidence describes)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged by this patch. Section 6.14 vise mechanism still forecloses every published escape route.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The patch was narrow (single character change in two files) and the only thing being verified is that .024 was fully replaced by .022 — confirmed by grep across data/ and docs/.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py automation for WIN-014. Citation-only patch does not introduce or remove any automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c7. Standard physics offers no positive prediction for the 2009 SG null; the dome interprets the null as confirming an electromagnetic-not-gravitational mechanism, which is a positive claim, not a relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c7. 2009 measurements published (Sun et al. attributed 2010 / Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013) before dome incorporated WIN.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c7. The null is adopted from literature; κ-prediction in our refutation derives Δg from dome κ but the WIN itself adopts a literature reading.

kernel_of_truth.description: Verification scope only — no new kernel-of-truth to surface. The Kim & Chang 2018 paper (data/wins.json:014:detail_evidence p3) does establish that eclipse-magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories are a real, statistically-documented phenomenon over a 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble — a genuine empirical record the dome can legitimately cite. The dome correctly recognized that eclipses produce measurable geomagnetic signatures.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing a real eclipse-magnetic signature does not validate WIN-014: the dome's own κ-coupling (WIN-012, data/wins.json:012) then converts those 5–15 nT signatures into a predicted Δg of 3–9 µGal at SG-co-located stations, which the 2009 Chinese campaign measured as zero (data/wins.json:014:detail_evidence p3). The kernel is real; the κ-arithmetic against it fails.

verification_checks.evidence: detail_verdict_text 869 chars matches c7 exactly. verdict still 'Refuted by Data'. detail_evidence length now 2407 (vs 2314 in c7) — the +93 delta predates ISS-1999 (it comes from EXP-358 citation-replacement that landed earlier in the same decider run), not from the .024→.022 narrow fix itself.

deeper_analysis: This is a narrow verification of a 1-character (digit) DOI fix. The hole introduced by EXP-358 PATCH-358-B/D (Kim & Chang citation with wrong DOI .024 pointing to Sadeghi & Emami paper) and caught by the WIN-013-014-SEC-6.14-EXP358-verification curmudgeon review is now closed: data/wins.json:014:detail_evidence carries .022 and no .024 remnants exist anywhere in data/ or docs/. The corrected DOI resolves to the actual Kim & Chang 2018 paper (verified via web search against ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K and topic/methodology matching detail_evidence verbatim). No new holes introduced by the patch. The c7 unresolved holes (H0 Tomás-resolves-wrong-paper, H1 dual-baseline ambiguity, H3 SG-site-coverage, H5 Crossley URL/year mismatch, H7 verdict-taxonomy hybrid) are out of scope for this verification — they remain open via their respective ISSs and should be tracked separately by the decider. The H2 minor (sixteen vs seventeen years) appears fixed in current text ('run seventeen years earlier' present in detail_evidence p4). Note that the H0 Tomás cite from c7 has been entirely supplanted by the new Kim & Chang citation via EXP-358 — decider should verify any open ISS tracking the Tomás bug is closed-as-superseded.

summary_for_decider: Queue_id 339 (WIN-014 ISS-1999 DOI .024→.022 verification): PASS. Patch landed cleanly across data/wins.json, data/sections.json, and rendered docs/index.html. Web-confirmed DOI .022 = Kim & Chang 2018 (PII S0273117718300656, ADS 2018AdSpR..61.2040K). No new holes. Five open holes from c7 (H0 superseded by EXP-358 citation swap; H1, H3, H5, H7 remain) are out-of-scope for this verification — track separately. Recommendation: close ISS-1999. Batch-position 1 of 2 (proceeding to SEC-6.14 next; PRED-106 deferred to singleton — see batch report).
